channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Blocked Drain, 20th May
item.description:
A drain opposite a junction of Folly Lane and St Paul's Road is blocked and has been fir a number of months. I reported this blockage a,week or so after a young lad tragically lost his life after being hit by a car whilst riding a motorised hire scooter. It is my view that he may have tried to avoid the surface water and therefore was struck by the car through no fault of his own. Following this previous report I received an email indicating that the blockage had been addressed and cleared...IT CLEARLY HAS NOT! Can this please be addressed as a matter of urgency before another tragically accident happens...I regularly use a bike along this route and have had to head into on coming traffic to avoid the surface water.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Saint Paul's Road, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road Subsiding on Oakfield Road, 20th May
item.description:
Road subsiding outside no 31 Oakfield Road. Collecting water.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 43,45, Oakfield Road, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: No rain in 24 hours - water has increased or remained the same

item.title: Pothole previously filled open again, 20th May
item.description:
Opposite 165
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 165, Hewlett Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham

item.title: Blocked drain on rosd, 20th May
item.description:
Blocked drain on road post resurfacing. Causing flooding
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leckhampton Hill, Coberley, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing signs - Brockhampton Lane, 19th May
item.description:
The poles and light fittings for illuminated signs on both sides of Brockhampton Lane are in place, but there are no signs on them. My guess is they should be 40mph speed limit signs when approaching Hyde Lane, and National Speed Limit signs in the other direction.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockhampton Lane, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole around edge of street drain, outside blenheim court, 19th May
item.description:
In Back Lane, on left side of the road going from library/school towards North Street.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Back Lane, Winchcombe, Winchcombe, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass on the roundabout is too high, you can't see motorbikes!, 19th May
item.description:
The roundabout grass is too high. When pulling out you can't see motorbikes, making it extremely dangerous
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The tarmac surrounding a large inspection chamber cover appears to be sinking, 19th May
item.description:
See map. The inspection chamber cover is dropping into the tarmac and seemingly getting steadily worse. Please follow up and inspect.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tatchley Lane, Lynworth, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wait time on toucan crossing is too long, 19th May
item.description:
Please can you fix the wait time on the toucan crossing on College road connecting the cycle and pedestrian path through Sandford Park. The wait time from pressing the button to cross is usually around 90 seconds - even when it has not been used recently. This is too long. It should be 30 seconds max in that situation. The long wait time leads to people crossing without waiting for the green man. This is potentially risky given the proximity of the junction, and frustrating for drivers who find themselves waiting at a red light when the pedestrians/ cyclists have already got bored of waiting and crossed without the signal.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish not being collected regularly, 19th May
item.description:
Up until very recently, the rubbish from Harrington House was collected every Thursday morning at approx 05:30. This has now stopped for whatever reason and the rubbish is piling up outside of the bins and is left in black bags for seagulls and other vermin to scavenge. Please can this be flagged as a potential health hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 180, Hatherley Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pot holes on road near caravan site, 19th May
item.description:
Near caravan site on Cursey lane. All of Cursey lane needs assessing as pot holes and worn road all down the road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cursey Lane, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped sofa has joined the dumped suitcase from Saturday., 19th May
item.description:
More rubbish in the usual location. Why does the council do absolutely nothing to prevent this repeated criminal activity happening here time and time again?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Pates Avenue, Kingsditch, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sign Frame and 2 x Sandbags left on island, 19th May
item.description:
Sign Frame and 2 x Sandbags left on island - have been here for some time.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Manhole erupting water, 19th May
item.description:
By the pedestrian crossing on Bishops Cleeve bypass, adjacent to Stoke Road. Apparently unsanitary. Pedestrian crossing unusable

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A435, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 19th May
item.description:
Customer reports that the current refuse collection vehicle may be stopped at the location due to the overhanging trees making contact with their mirrors/the vehicle. Please can these be cut back.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing manhole during heavy rain, 19th May
item.description:
Outside Andrews estate agents in the pavement, near the alley towards tesco

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Church Road, Bishop's Cleeve, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The drain is blocked problem, 19th May
item.description:
Blocked drain and seems to be a lot of stuff built up inside the drain. Needs clearing
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No street lighting in the entire section of road until you reach the corner, 19th May
item.description:
The whole stretch of road from the traffic bollard to the corner has no street lighting - it is therefore total darkness and a very dangerous section of road where people are at risk of attack or accident - it needs some lighting added as soon as possible, as there has been nothing there for over 5 years now!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Farm Lane, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects - Vegetation problem, 19th May
item.description:
PLEASE CUT BACK HEDGE TO BOUNDARY ON BOTH SIDES OF FOOTPATH 7m FOOTPATH CLOSURE MARKED (Overgrown Hedge)
Report on FixMyStreet
